user_feedback:
🌸 p5.js says: There's an error as "getWorldCenter" could not be called as a function (on line 96 in entities.js [http://[::]:1415/games/single_prompt_with_testing/game_190191/sample_0/entities.js:96:35]).
Verify whether "s" has "getWorldCenter" in it and check the spelling, letter-casing (JavaScript is case-sensitive) and its type.
For more: https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Errors/Not_a_function#What_went_wrong
p5.js:60084 ▶️ Error at line 96 in "Target.checkCollision" in entities.js (http://[::]:1415/games/single_prompt_with_testing/game_190191/sample_0/entities.js:96:35)

entities.js:96 Uncaught TypeError: s.getWorldCenter is not a function
    at Target.checkCollision (entities.js:96:35)
    at updateGameLogic (game.js:87:28)

analysis:
- Issue identified: The `Target.checkCollision` method in `entities.js` calls `s.getWorldCenter(body.pos, body.angle)` on every shape `s` of a physics body. However, `BoxShape` (a subclass of `Shape`) does not implement `getWorldCenter`, causing a crash when a body with a box shape (like 'l' or 'i') interacts with a target.
- Files affected: `physics_core.js`.
- Changes needed: Move the `getWorldCenter` method from `CircleShape` to the base `Shape` class so that all shape types (including `BoxShape`) inherit it. This ensures the method is available for all shapes as required by the gameplay logic.
